innerText:
Every song here is two numbers: how loud, how fast. So is your taste. Turn the amber taste arrow and watch each song's match get built: the loud product plus the fast product, stacked into one bar. Then grab Night Drive's round handle and change its size.

Click any bar or arrow to inspect it. The dashed lines are that arrow's two entries (its loudness and its speed), the numbers the recipe multiplies.

the whole recipe, live for Night Drive:
loud 0.77·0.94 + fast 0.64·0.34 = 0.72 + 0.22 = 0.9
multiply the matched entries, add the products. The lilac slice of each bar is its loud product, the mint slice its fast product; stacked, they are the score.

Why this matters: A song that is simply louder and faster everywhere piles up score without matching your mix. A raw meter overrates intense tracks, so a recommender built on it keeps pushing whatever is biggest.

Click any bar or arrow to inspect it. The dashed lines are that arrow's two entries (its loudness and its speed), the numbers the recipe multiplies.

the whole recipe, live for Night Drive:
loud 0.34·1.88 + fast 0.94·0.68 = 0.64 + 0.64 = 1.3
multiply the matched entries, add the products. The lilac slice of each bar is its loud product, the mint slice its fast product; stacked, they are the score.
